原文:

SUNDARAM YADAV

Posted on Mar 5

Here are some common API errors that you should be aware of when working with APIs:
400 Bad Request - This error is typically returned when the client has made a request that the server is unable to process. This can occur if the request is missing required parameters or if the parameters are formatted incorrectly.

401 Unauthorized - This error occurs when the client attempts to access an API endpoint without the necessary authentication credentials.

403 Forbidden - This error occurs when the client has authenticated successfully but is not authorized to access the requested resource.

404 Not Found - This error occurs when the requested resource is not available on the server.

405 Method Not Allowed - This error occurs when the client attempts to use an HTTP method that is not allowed for the requested resource.

500 Internal Server Error - This error is typically returned when the server encounters an unexpected error while processing the request.

503 Service Unavailable - This error is typically returned when the server is unable to handle the request due to high traffic or maintenance.

Items.Title: 从 React 切换到 Svelte：为什么是时候采取行动了 | Switching from React to Svelte: Why it's Time to Make the Move
Items.Description:
各位开发者，早上好！ 今天，我想和大家谈谈我已经思考了一段时间的一个重要话题：为什么是时候从 React 切换到 Svelte。

现在，我知道你们中的许多人可能对 React 感到满意。 毕竟，多年来它一直是前端开发的热门选择。 但请听我说完。 首先，Svelte 比 React 快得多。 这意味着您的应用程序将更加敏捷且响应更快，从而为您的用户提供更好的用户体验。

其次，Svelte 比 React 更容易学习和使用。 使用 React，您需要学习整个生态系统的工具和库才能开始使用，这对初学者来说可能是难以承受的。 另一方面，Svelte 具有更简单、更直观的语法，使其更容易快速启动和运行。

但这不仅仅是关于易用性和性能。 例如，Svelte 的反应式编程模型允许您编写比 React 更简洁、更具表现力的代码。 现在，我知道从 React 切换到 Svelte 似乎是一项艰巨的任务。 但相信我，这是值得的。 借助 Svelte，您将能够以更少的代码和更少的麻烦构建速度更快、响应更快的应用程序。 作为额外的奖励，您将学习到一种在 Web 开发社区中迅速普及的尖端技术。

因此，让我们尝试一下，从 React 切换到 Svelte。 您的用户（和您的理智）会为此感谢您。 编码愉快！

一旦暂停，jlarky 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，jlarky 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jlarky 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jlarky 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 jlarky：

降低 jlarky 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jlarky 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

   

Good morning, fellow developers! Today, I want to talk to you about an important topic that's been on my mind for a while now: why it's time to switch from React to Svelte.

Now, I know that many of you are probably comfortable with React. After all, it's been a popular choice for front-end development for years. But hear me out. Svelte is a newer, more modern approach to web development that offers some key advantages over React.

Firstly, Svelte is much faster than React. Svelte compiles your code into highly optimized vanilla JavaScript, resulting in much smaller bundle sizes and faster load times. This means that your applications will be snappier and more responsive, providing a better user experience for your users.

Secondly, Svelte is much easier to learn and use than React. With React, you need to learn a whole ecosystem of tools and libraries just to get started, which can be overwhelming for beginners. Svelte, on the other hand, has a much simpler and more intuitive syntax, making it easier to get up and running quickly.

But it's not just about ease of use and performance. Svelte also offers some unique features that are not available in React. For example, Svelte's reactive programming model allows you to write code that is both more concise and more expressive than in React. Svelte also offers powerful animations and transitions out of the box, which can make your applications feel more polished and professional.

Now, I know that switching from React to Svelte can seem like a daunting task. But trust me, it's worth it. With Svelte, you'll be able to build faster, more responsive applications with less code and less hassle. And as an added bonus, you'll be learning a cutting-edge technology that is quickly gaining popularity in the web development community.

So let's take the plunge and switch from React to Svelte. Your users (and your sanity) will thank you for it. Happy coding!
